summary: Russian forces used the "Yolka" air defense drone to kinetically intercept a Ukrainian fixed-wing UAV, with operators noted for clear communication during the engagement. In Donetsk region, Ukrainian forces killed Russian propagandist Evgeny Nikolayev ("Hayduk") on March 10 using an FPV drone, though Russian sources claim he died from a mine explosion. Operators from the "Sever" group deployed "Molniya" drones to destroy Ukrainian UAV control points and pursued survivors with FPV drones in Korenek, Sumy region. The "Rubicon" Center reported 11 targets eliminated on March 16, including a Ukrainian "Gepard" self-propelled anti-aircraft gun and operations against fixed-wing UAVs. A ZALA Lancet RUK crew destroyed an American M777 howitzer in Sumy region after detection by a Z-16 UAV. Ukrainian FPV pilots employed tactics such as targeting dismounted Russian soldiers and using simultaneous strikes, while Russian forces adapted by using log-periodic directional antennas on "Molniya" drones for increased range and jamming resistance.
